source: Reserve Bank of India (RBI) Draft Guidance on Regulatory Principles for Model Risk Management (MRM) in Financial Institutions, 2026

rules.requirement: Financial Institutions must implement mandatory real-time kill switches on all active AI systems and transaction models.
rules.description: Any automated agent or system must be subject to immediate suspension by human compliance officers in the event of anomalies, risk threshold breaches, or suspected compromise, ensuring subsequent operations are blocked immediately.
rules.howTrustMeshSatisfies: Implemented via kill_switch_override tool, which records an immutable override log and halts verification/scoring for the target agent/mandate instantly.

rules.requirement: AI and automated decision models must operate without black-box logic for high-value clearing or risk scoring.
rules.description: All risk assessments, blocks, or reviews must have clearly documented and explainable decision trails detailing specific risk factors triggered and their respective weights.
rules.howTrustMeshSatisfies: Uses a transparent, rule-weighted scoring model (Merchant Scope +30, Geo Mismatch +20, Velocity +25, Invalid Signature +100) instead of opaque deep learning models.
rules.rbiSection: Section 5.1 - Model Explainability and Transparency Standards

rules.requirement: Transactions marked as medium-to-high risk must be routed to compliance officer queues for manual oversight.
rules.description: System-generated recommendations must not bypass human verification when risk thresholds are exceeded, limiting automated clearing to strictly low-risk segments.
rules.howTrustMeshSatisfies: Transactions with risk scores between 50 and 74 are marked as 'review' (HITL queue), whereas scores 75+ are blocked and <50 approved.
rules.rbiSection: Section 4.5 - Risk Escalation & Manual Oversight Integration

rules.requirement: Every decision, verification, and human override action must be written to an immutable, timestamped audit log.
rules.description: Maintains a sequentially ordered, append-only log that logs the actor, timestamp, action context, and parameters, ensuring tamper-evidence for regulatory examinations.
rules.howTrustMeshSatisfies: Writes every verify, score, and override event into audit-log://transactions with cryptographic hash-chaining (simulated hash-chain).
